core_mechanism: an iterative back-and-forth cascade (feedback removal) between two mutually-dependent coupled systems, formalized as a percolation transition with a sharp critical connectivity threshold below which the coupled system fragments completely and above which a giant mutually-connected component survives
brief_reason: The abstract describes failures in one network triggering failures in a coupled network and propagating back and forth until total fragmentation, with an analytically derived critical average degree (<k_c>=2.445) separating collapse from survival — a coupled-percolation threshold cascade.
